channel.title: Unhedged Summary
channel.description: AI-generated audio summaries of FT Unhedged podcast episodes for personal market briefing use.
channel.itunes:author: Eric
channel.itunes:summary: AI-generated audio summaries of FT Unhedged podcast episodes for personal market briefing use.

item.title: China Shock 2.0
item.description:
## 1. 5-bullet executive summary
- **“China Shock 2.0” is no longer about low-end manufacturing**: China has moved from widgets/textiles into high-value sectors such as EVs, solar panels, batteries, machine tools, shipbuilding, rail, and supercomputing. - **Europe and Southeast Asia are most exposed**: Germany’s auto and capital-goods base, and Southeast Asia’s industrialisation model, are being pressured by Chinese overcapacity and export competitiveness. - **China’s industrial model is structurally different**: heavy subsidies, local-government production targets, VAT incentives and state-directed capital create firms that are highly competitive globally, even if not always economically efficient. - **Green technology is now a geopolitical lever**: China has solar-panel capacity estimated at roughly double global demand and dominates key parts of the EV/battery/renewables supply chain, raising dependency risks for Europe and emerging markets. - **Iran/oil-market disruption could str
